description: Evaluate campaign brief, content generation, positioning, and launch planning quality

scenarios.query: Create a campaign brief for launching our new AI-powered analytics feature to enterprise customers
expected_behaviors: Creates SMART objective with specific numeric targets
Defines detailed ICP with job titles and pain points
Includes at least 3 messaging pillars with proof points
Specifies channels appropriate for enterprise (LinkedIn, email, webinar)
Includes measurable success metrics with baselines
Defines testable hypotheses
Runs validation script before completing
Cites at least one framework from Domain Expertise Canon
Runs context adaptation protocol and identifies Enterprise GTM signals
quality_gates: Objective contains numeric target and deadline
Target audience has company size and pain points
Marketing thinking checklist is referenced or completed

scenarios.query: Create a content pack for our new collaboration feature targeting startup CTOs
expected_behaviors: LinkedIn post is professional and under 3000 chars
Twitter content is thread format (numbered)
Email has compelling subject line and clear CTA
All content aligned to startup CTO pain points
Each piece has distinct CTA appropriate to channel
Context adaptation identifies Growth stage / Developer audience signals
Applies PAS or AIDA framework to structure copy

scenarios.query: Create a 5-email nurture sequence for trial users who haven't converted after 7 days

context.segment: Users who created project but no team invites

expected_behaviors: Email 1 addresses specific user behavior (no invites)
Progression from education to urgency over sequence
Includes personalization variables ({{first_name}}, {{project_name}})
Each email has single, clear CTA
Final emails include urgency (trial ending)
Maps sequence to Hook Model stages (trigger, action, reward, investment)
References AARRR framework to identify activation as the bottleneck
quality_gates: All emails have subject line and body

scenarios.query: Create a competitive battlecard for our CRM against Salesforce for mid-market deals
expected_behaviors: Clear differentiation points highlighted
Objection handlers for common Salesforce advantages
Specific landmine questions to set
Proof points with customer examples
Win strategies tailored to mid-market
Acknowledges at least one genuine Salesforce strength
Includes trap questions that expose Salesforce weakness in mid-market context
quality_gates: Acknowledges at least one competitor strength (no one-sided narratives)

scenarios.query: Create a positioning document for an AI-powered code review tool targeting engineering leaders at mid-stage startups

context.product: AI code review assistant that learns team patterns
context.target_market: Engineering leaders at Series B-D startups (50-500 engineers)
unique_capabilities: Learns team-specific coding patterns and conventions
Reduces review cycle time from days to hours
proof_points: 3 design partners reduced review time by 60%
One customer caught 12 architectural violations in first week

expected_behaviors: Applies April Dunford positioning framework (competitive alternatives, unique attributes, value, best-fit customers, market category)
Defines competitive alternatives from the customer's perspective, not the company's
Maps unique attributes to customer outcomes, not features
Specifies best-fit customers with triggering events and current workarounds
Chooses market category type (existing, adjacent, or new) with rationale
Includes proof points with type classification (customer, data, analyst, award)
Defines 3-5 messaging pillars each backed by specific proof
Runs context adaptation protocol and identifies Pre-PMF to Growth stage transition
Queries org memory for prior positioning and competitive intelligence
Passes the 'so what?' test -- every attribute connects to a customer outcome
quality_gates: proof_points has >= 3 entries with type classification
messaging_pillars has 3-5 entries each with headline, supporting_copy, and proof
No unsubstantiated superlatives (industry-leading, best-in-class without data)
April Dunford framework is explicitly cited as the structural foundation

scenarios.query: Create a Tier 1 launch plan for our platform rebrand and new enterprise product line

context.product: Developer productivity platform rebranding from DevBoost to Velocity
context.timeline: 12 weeks total (4 week tease, launch day, 8 week activation)
constraints: Must not disrupt existing customer workflows
Developer community is skeptical of enterprise pivots

expected_behaviors: Classifies as Tier 1 launch (rebrand + new product line)
Includes all four phases: tease, announce, activate, sustain
Specifies >= 5 channels with distinct roles, timing, owners, and budgets
Includes enablement materials (internal FAQ, customer FAQ, demo script, objection handlers)
Defines success metrics by phase (tease, announce, activate, sustain)
Includes contingency plan for each phase
Includes rollback criteria
Addresses the three distinct audiences with tailored messaging per audience
Applies Category Design framework for the rebrand narrative
Applies StoryBrand framework for the brand narrative arc
Identifies analyst brief as a prerequisite and hands off to analyst-brief artifact
Addresses the developer community skepticism constraint explicitly
Runs context adaptation protocol and identifies Enterprise GTM + Developer audience signals
Queries org memory for prior brand assets and customer communication templates
quality_gates: timeline includes tease, announce, activate, and sustain phases
channels >= 5 with name, content_description, timing, owner, and budget
enablement_materials includes internal_faq, customer_facing_faq, demo_script, and objection_handlers
rollback_criteria is defined with specific thresholds
At least two frameworks from Domain Expertise Canon are cited
Cross-agent handoffs identified (design-agent for visual rebrand, sales-agent for enablement, analyst-brief for analyst relations)
30/60/90-day sustain plan is present (no Launch and Abandon anti-pattern)

common_failures: Missing messaging pillars or proof points
Positioning based on features rather than customer outcomes
No competitive context or one-sided narratives

key_improvements: Detailed ICP with pain points and triggers
